channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Kiln Roadmap Unveils Content Plans For 2026
item.content:encoded:
A roadmap has been revealed for Kiln ahead of its release tomorrow, April 23, 2026. It showcases a variety of content that fans can expect across the first few weeks after launch, as well as some general plans throughout the rest of 2026.

The upcoming online party brawler has players sculpting ceramic pots, which they can then possess and use as a means to douse the enemies’ Kiln. It looks like there’s a lot of customisation on offer, and the roadmap details that more variety will be on the way.

Kiln Roadmap Reveals Year-Long Plans

Kiln will launch with five maps and its core game mode, Quench, but the devs at Double Fine Productions have a steady stream of content planned for the title. It’s been revealed that every week for the first month after Kiln launches, there will be new updates for fans to unpack.

Week one kicks things off with a Dinosaur Decoration Pack, adding a selection of new cosmetics to deck out your pottery with. The following week will add another map to the roster, before it’s back to more cosmetics with weeks three and four.

Looking beyond the first month, the first Feature Update will be Mission Criti-Bowl, which will include two new maps, as well as a host of Progression System additions like Missions and a Pot Journal.

It begins to get a bit vague heading into Fall and Winter, with the key update being new ways to play. That’s likely more game modes, or perhaps an expansion onto other platforms—only time will tell.

Until then, the level cap has risen to 50 for the game’s full release, giving fans tons of content to unlock and grind towards in the opening weeks. Remember, the game releases tomorrow, April 23, so you’ll be able to get stuck in very soon.

How does the Kiln roadmap look to you? Will it be enough content to keep fans engaged? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out Nintendo being sued over tariffs, as well as Ben 10 joining Fortnite.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Pokémon TCG Pocket B3 Next Expansion—Leaked Release Date, Explained
item.content:encoded:
The rumors are already Poliswirling around Pokémon TCG Pocket B3, as it’s the next expansion outed. This means a bigger set, lots of cards, and more duplicates than your trade pile knows what to do with.

I hope you’ve had fun with Mega Shine. To be honest, even if you’re a free-to-play user, you should have completed it by now, as it was a small enough collection. As always, a new month means another significant update, and it seems like Pokémon TCG Pocket B3 is the next expansion.

When Is Pokémon TCG Pocket B3 Expansion’s Reveal Date?

According to leaks, the Pokémon TCG Pocket B3 expansion will be unveiled with a new trailer on April 23.

Who doesn’t love those flashy Pokémon TCG Pocket trailers? They’re a great advert, and there’s always a nice tease of special cards. Pocket has Mega ex Cards and Ultra Beasts, and there’s so much variety compared to launch.

  • More Insider Gaming: How to Sign Up For Pokémon Center Early Access

However, I’d argue the game does need a new, big feature soon to inject some life into it. Whether B3 will be bringing that remains to be seen, but you shouldn’t have to wait long to find out if the reports are right.

Pokémon TCG Pocket B3 Expansion’s Release Date, Explained

The B3 expansion trailer should confirm the rumored April 28 release date for the new set.

As with most sets, the trailer is dropping around the third week of the month, and then the set arrives during the final week. It’s more clockwork than the central objectives of a mainline Pokémon game.

  • More Insider Gaming: When Will Pokémon Champions Add More Pokémon?

We still don’t know what the theme for the set is going to be, or who’s in it, though. As it’s not an ‘a’ set, it should mean a couple of hundred cards should feature, and there’ll be a lot of incentive to open the packs throughout April and May.

Stay tuned as we’ll bring you the first news on B3, and the debut trailer should confirm everything suspected and leaked so far.

Do you still play Pokémon TCG Pocket daily? Is it losing its appeal, or do certain changes need to happen? Share your thoughts and more via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Also, check out our hub on when Pokémon Champions is coming to Mobile.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Xbox CEO Says Idea of a Mobile Store is “Not Dead”
item.content:encoded:
Asha Sharma has clarified that, even though the Xbox Mobile Store page is down, the idea is not dead yet.

The CEO of Xbox recently announced significant changes to Xbox Game Pass, including a price reduction and the confirmation that Call of Duty games will not be available as Day One releases on the service. Now, Asha Sharma has discussed another area of the business, the Xbox Mobile Store, indicating that it may still have some potential for growth.

Xbox Mobile Store is Down, But the Idea of It Is Not Dead

The issue with the Xbox Mobile Store surfaced recently when players realized the store page was no longer functional and was showing an error. Jez Corden addressed this removal on X (formerly Twitter) and received a response from Asha Sharma. Sharma stated that an amicus was filed three weeks ago, arguing that “mobile competition still matters,” and that they want the future of play to be “more open”. The CEO added that while they are still learning, “the idea of an Xbox mobile store is not dead.”

In 2024, former Xbox President Sarah Bond announced that an Xbox mobile gaming store was in the works. The goal was to bring their first-party portfolio to the store, along with games like Candy Crush and Minecraft. They had decided that the store would be web-based at first, to make it “accessible across all devices, all countries, no matter what, and independent of the policies of closed ecosystem stores.”

In other news, a new claim suggests that Xbox Game Pass is looking to become more flexible, with a ‘pick your own plan’ option under consideration. What are your thoughts on the Xbox CEO stating that the idea of an Xbox Mobile Store is not dead? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Blood of Dawnwalker developers revealing the release date next week. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Are New Releases Still on Xbox Game Pass? Day One Releases, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Xbox Game Pass has proven to be a rollercoaster ride for subscribers, with multiple price adjustments, revamps, and overhauls taking place, and the most recent changes have raised questions about the status of day-one releases.

Game Pass as a service has thrived on offering new releases as soon as they drop for no additional cost, featuring major franchises like Forza Horizon, Call of Duty, and Gears of War.

Changes are afoot in April 2026, however, following a welcome slash to the cost, though it does mean there are some new day one rules to get your head around.

Are New Releases Still on Xbox Game Pass?

Yes, the majority of first-party games releasing on Xbox platforms will be available day one upon release, but there are some exceptions.

Game Pass is a major selling point for the Microsoft consoles, particularly in a period where their major console releases have not had as much impact as the likes of PlayStation and Nintendo, and removing new releases would significantly alter the deal.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Xbox Game Pass Prices Following Game Pass Ultimate Price Drop, Explained

As such, the majority of first-party games on Xbox will be available day one, though the usual limitations of subscription tiers apply, with only Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass subscribers receiving the benefit.

Game Pass Premium subscribers will be entitled to those releases within one year of the release date, with the structure not being altered in the most recent price changes.

What Games Won’t be on Xbox Game Pass Day One?

Call of Duty titles will not be available day one through Xbox Game Pass, Microsoft announced, meaning Black Ops 7 is the sole title having had this treatment.

Microsoft’s takeover of Activision Blizzard opened the doors for Call of Duty titles to be included on the Game Pass service upon release, though this significantly impacted sales as a result.

  • More Insider Gaming: How Does Xbox Game Pass Ultimate Price Drop Affect Call of Duty?

As such, the reduction in price for Game Pass comes alongside confirmation that future Call of Duty titles will not release day one on the Game Pass service, but could still feature later down the line.

Call of Duty is the only exception to the day one approach with Game Pass, at least for now, but the changes do show that Microsoft is not afraid to switch it up, and fans will hope that similar moves aren’t made with other franchises like Halo and Gears of War.

Is the removal of Call of Duty games day one on Xbox Game Pass a big loss? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Game Pass, read about claims of a new ‘pick your plan’ formula to the service, and check out all games leaving Game Pass in April 2026.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/are-new-releases-still-on-xbox-game-pass-day-one-releases-explained/

item.title: Nintendo Is Being Sued Over Tariff Refunds
item.content:encoded:
A class action lawsuit has been filed against Nintendo over pending tariff refunds to the company.

First reported by Stephen Totilo at Game File, two gamers—Gregory Hoffert of California and Prashant Sharan of Washington—are suing the company, believing that consumers should be the ones getting their money back. The suit was filed in the Western District court of Washington, the state where Nintendo of America is headquartered.

“Unless restrained by this Court, Nintendo stands to recover the same tariff payments twice—once from consumers through higher prices and again from the federal government through tariff refunds, including interest paid by the government on those funds,” the claim reads.

Totilo adds that the two “allege that Nintendo’s recovery of any tariff refunds would constitute unjust enrichment and violate Washington state consumer protection law.”

The suit seeks to represent all those in the United States who purchased any Nintendo products that had seen their prices increased between February 1, 2025 and February 24, 2026.

In March, Nintendo filed its own lawsuit against the United States government over Donald Trump’s tariffs. The tariffs were found to be unconstitutional, and the United States was to begin a process of refunding the money gained from companies.

After that lawsuit was filed, Totilo says Nintendo stepped around answering his question regarding whether the company would pass the refunds on to consumers.

Insider Gaming has reached out to Nintendo for comment on the lawsuit. Should a response be received, it will be added to this story.

What do you think about Nintendo being sued over the tariff refunds? Should customers be the ones to get the refunds? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Ubisoft canceling its Animal Crossing-inspired game Alterra. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Blood of Dawnwalker Showcase Announced for Next Week
item.content:encoded:
The Blood of Dawnwalker Road to Launch showcase has been announced, arriving on April 28, 2026, when players will finally receive a release date.

Rebel Wolves’ first chapter in a new role-playing saga, The Blood of Dawnwalker, was revealed back in January 2025 with a cinematic trailer. Since then, the developers have revealed more details and 20 minutes of gameplay, which felt like The Witcher with parkour. Now, a new showcase is set to arrive next week.

The Blood of Dawnwalker Release Date Will Be Revealed Next Week

A new announcement confirms that a Blood of Dawnwalker showcase, titled Road to Launch, is arriving on April 28. It will be a 45-minute session that reveals loads of content and additional details. From what’s been announced, we do know that Blood of Dawnwalker’s release date will be revealed at the event, along with new open-world gameplay, a story trailer, system requirements, developer insiders, music, and much more. Check out the teaser for the event below;

The Blood of Dawnwalker is an upcoming open-world dark fantasy action RPG, where you play as a Dawnwalker, a person who is “forever treading the line between the world of day and the realm of night”. The game is set in 14th-century Europe, where you are a human by day and a vampire by night, and you will need to fight for your humanity while using your new cursed powers to save your family.

The title features two distinct gameplay loops, in which you use different abilities, mysteries, and other means to achieve your goals depending on the time of day. Another new system is timing: moving forward every time you take on a quest means every action matters. The open world is pretty diverse as well, featuring lush forests, vast plains, treacherous swamps, steep mountaintops, and medieval settlements.

In other news, senior developers at Rebel Wolves highlighted their desire to make Blood of Dawnwalker about quality and not quantity. What are your thoughts on The Blood of Dawnwalker showcase and release date arriving next week? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about MindsEye’s ‘company sabotage’ mission arrives in the wake of lawsuits. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: How Does Xbox Game Pass Ultimate Price Drop Affect Call of Duty? All CoD Games Affected, Explained
item.content:encoded:
In a shocking twist, Asha Sharma revealed that the price of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate and PC was being reduced, effective immediately. It’s since garnered huge celebrations from the gaming community, but it did come at a cost…

To account for the drop in price, it’s been announced that Call of Duty games won’t be arriving day-and-date on Xbox Game Pass, ending a two-year stint since Xbox acquired Activision.

However, that presents a ton of questions regarding the future of the franchise on the service: what will happen to older CoD games on Game Pass, and will new ones be added in the future? Thankfully, Insider Gaming has all of the answers below, as Call of Duty’s Xbox Game Pass future is clarified.

Will New Call Of Duty Games Be Added To Xbox Game Pass?

Yes, new Call of Duty games will be added to Xbox Game Pass, though they won’t arrive day one as they have done in the past.

Instead, Asha Sharma confirmed that new Call of Duty titles will arrive around a year after their launch, so the following holiday period. As things stand, this will be exclusively for Xbox Game Pass Ultimate subscribers, and it’s unclear how long after the game’s release they could arrive for lower tiers like Premium or Essential—if at all.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Xbox Game Pass Prices Following Game Pass Ultimate Price Drop

Therefore, if you’re looking to play the latest Call of Duty when it releases, you’ll have to buy the game day and date separately, as it’ll no longer arrive on Xbox Game Pass at the same time.

Will Old Call Of Duty Games Remain On Xbox Game Pass?

Nothing’s being removed from the service, as Call of Duty titles currently on Xbox Game Pass will remain available for Ultimate subscribers.

There were concerns when the price drop was announced that if new Call of Duty titles were to be added later, would that mean older titles could be removed from the service, too?

  • More Insider Gaming: Xbox VP Clarifies ‘First-Party’ Project Helix Rumors

Thankfully, Asha was quick to clarify that detail for subscribers, as she confirmed that any Call of Duty title currently on Xbox Game Pass would remain there. That means you can continue grinding Black Ops 7 with no concern of the game being taken away—just make sure your wallet is ready for the new title this Winter.

What do you make of the Xbox Game Pass price drops? Is losing CoD as a day-one release a fair trade-off? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming guides, check out the following:

  • Uncharted 5 Release Date Rumors, Explained
  • Will GTA 6 Be Delayed Again in 2026? All Delays, Explained

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…
